description: Only agent_tool_result_cache has ENABLE/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + policy. All other tenant-scoped agents tables (agent_prompt_store, agent_narrative_store, agent_session, agent_stored_sub_agent, agent_tool_invocation, agent_iteration, agent_conversation, agent_message, agent_chat_session, agent_chat_message, agent_cost_event, agent_draft, agent_insight, agent_l3_preference, agent_scratchpad, agent_active_turn, agent_turn_sampling_decision, agent_tenant_budget, agent_user_budget, agent_rate_limit_counter, agent_rollout_config, agent_rollout_event, agent_shadow_run, agent_canary_query, agent_canary_run, agent_golden_trace, agent_schedule, agent_schedule_run) have no RLS.
evidence: grep -n 'ENABLE ROW LEVEL\|FORCE ROW LEVEL\|CREATE POLICY' 0000_initial.sql → only 3 hits, all for agents.agent_tool_result_cache (lines 1832-1834)
suggested_action: Add ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + tenant_isolation policy to every agents.* table that contains tenant_id. Squash into 0000_initial.sql per CLAUDE.md migration rules.

description: auditEmit does not populate on_behalf_of, via_delegation_id, or via_schedule_id fields in the agent.tool_called audit payload. These fields exist in the agents.agent_tool_invocation schema (line 417 via_schedule_id) but are not passed through RequestContext → auditFacade.recordEvent.
evidence: grep -n 'on_behalf_of\|via_delegation\|via_schedule' pipeline-steps.ts → no results; schema has viaScheduleId at line 417
suggested_action: Extend RequestContext with optional onBehalfOf/viaDelegationId/viaScheduleId. Pass through auditEmit's payload. Adds traceability for delegated/scheduled tool calls.

description: OTel span attribute audit_row_id is set to undefined on both the invoke step and audit-emit step. A TODO comment explicitly marks this as pending plan 07 (two locations: lines ~911 and ~1022).
evidence: grep -n 'TODO-plan-07\|audit_row_id' tool-gateway.ts → lines 911 and 1022: 'audit_row_id will be available once plan 07 exposes the audit record ID'
suggested_action: Wire audit_row_id once plan 07 (Audit trail) is shipped. Currently renders all gateway spans incomplete for correlation queries. Acceptable as known cross-plan dependency.

description: Plan 01 §8 specifies agent_tool_cache_hit_ratio{tenant_id, sub_agent_key} as a gauge. Implementation uses agent_tool_cache_lookup_total{tenant_id, tool_name, outcome} counter. sub_agent_key label was intentionally dropped (cardinality concern). The deviation is documented but not reflected in the plan.
evidence: gateway-metrics.ts: counter named agent_tool_cache_lookup_total; plan §8 specifies gauge agent_tool_cache_hit_ratio
suggested_action: Update plan 01 §8 to document the adopted metric shape (counter with outcome label) as the canonical form. No code change needed if the rationale stands.

description: resolveMenuFor screen-relevance filter has a TODO comment: '// TODO plan-02: refine screen-relevance heuristic'. The current implementation is a stub; the production menu scoping algorithm is deferred.
evidence: tool-registry.ts line 227: '// TODO plan-02: refine screen-relevance heuristic'
suggested_action: Complete the screen-relevance heuristic as part of plan 02 delivery. Define the matching algorithm (e.g., tool screen tag ⊆ current surface screens). Add unit tests covering cross-screen tool scoping.

description: agent_tool_embedding stores embeddings as JSONB (number[]) instead of pgvector's vector type. Plan 02.5 §3 requires 'vector index appropriate to the driver for ANN nearest-neighbor'. A JSONB column cannot hold a pgvector index — ANN queries fall back to JS-level cosine.ts linear scan.
evidence: agent-tool-embedding.schema.ts: embedding column is jsonb not vector; cosine.ts implements pure-JS similarity; no ANN index in migration
suggested_action: Add pgvector extension, change embedding column to vector(1536), add ivfflat or hnsw index on (embedding vector_cosine_ops). This is a hard requirement for production-scale tool retrieval performance.

description: SubAgentRegistry.boot() validates R-02.6..R-02.9 at module init. Boot-fail-loud pattern is correctly implemented (SubAgentRegistryValidationError thrown). No deviation detected.
evidence: sub-agent-registry.ts: SubAgentRegistryValidationError class; boot() validates key uniqueness, toolScope overlap, and required fields
suggested_action: No action required.

description: In the 'completed' branch of buildSubAgentOutput, usageTotals is hardcoded to ZERO_USAGE instead of using the passed-in usageTotals parameter. The ceiling_hit and errored branches correctly use the parameter. This silently drops token/cost accounting for all successful sub-agent runs.
evidence: sub-agent-runner.ts line 160: 'usageTotals: ZERO_USAGE' (hardcoded constant, not the destructured opts.usageTotals)
suggested_action: Change line 160 from 'usageTotals: ZERO_USAGE' to 'usageTotals' to use the destructured variable. Add a test asserting that usageTotals passes through on the completed path.

description: SubAgentRunnerAdapter.run() calls buildSubAgentOutput with rawStructured: {} and all-zero signals. The Vercel AI SDK tool loop (real ReAct execution) is not wired. Every sub-agent invocation returns kind='errored' (empty object fails schema) or kind='completed' with empty structured output. No actual tool calls are made during an agent turn.
evidence: sub-agent-runner-adapter.ts lines 72-88: rawStructured: {}, signals: all-zero, summary: '[adapter] ${subAgentKey}'
suggested_action: This is a critical feature gap — the agents module cannot produce real answers. Wire the Vercel AI SDK tool loop per plan 03 §4. This is the primary blocker for production readiness.

description: SynthesizerAdapter.synthesize() uses only deterministic pure functions (detectContradiction, buildCitations, buildDisclosureStatements) with no LLM call. Plan 03 §9 requires an LLM-based synthesis pass for narrative generation. The adapter produces mechanical concatenation, not a coherent natural language answer.
evidence: synthesizer-adapter.ts lines 23-51: no LLM call; content = summaries.join(' ')
suggested_action: Wire the LLM synthesis call (plan 03 §9). The pure functions in synthesizer.ts are correct scaffolding — they should be called from the LLM prompt construction, not used as the final output.

description: Plan 12 R-12.6 requires canDo('agent.iterative') permission gate before iterative dispatch. Implementation correctly calls kernelQueryFacade.canDo(userId, 'agent.iterative', { tenantId }) at line 648 before IterativeOrchestrator.execute().
evidence: router-session-orchestrator.ts line 648: 'const allowed = await this.kernelQueryFacade.canDo(userId, 'agent.iterative', { tenantId })'
suggested_action: No action required.

description: Plan 12 R-12.4a requires iterative plans on inline surface to be retried with a bounded hint. Implementation correctly detects topology=iterative+surface=inline at line 351 and calls _applyInlineSurfaceGuard which retries with hint and disambiguates on persistent iterative emission.
evidence: router-session-orchestrator.ts lines 351, 541-604: inline surface guard with retry + hard disambiguation
suggested_action: No action required.

description: Plan 12 §5 specifies iteration caps: 10 interactive, 20 async. SURFACE_MAX_ITERATIONS is correctly set to {interactive: 10, async: 20}.
evidence: iterative-orchestrator.ts: SURFACE_MAX_ITERATIONS = { interactive: 10, async: 20 }
suggested_action: No action required.

description: The R-01.19 aggregate output detector uses a fixed key set (average, avg, count, counts, max, min, sum, total, totals). Tools returning aggregates with non-standard keys (e.g. 'headcount', 'budget_utilized') will not trigger the minGroupSize violation check, silently bypassing R-01.19.
evidence: drift-rules.ts lines 56-66: AGGREGATE_KEYS = new Set(['average','avg','count','counts','max','min','sum','total','totals'])
suggested_action: Extend AGGREGATE_KEYS or allow tool authors to explicitly tag aggregate outputs in AgentToolMeta (e.g. agent.isAggregate: true) as a more reliable signal than key-name heuristics.

description: window-builder.ts line 107 notes: 'MVP compressed tier: concatenation placeholder (Phase 4 will call the nano summarizer)'. The compressed tier of the context window (messages 4-13) is concatenated verbatim instead of being summarized by gpt-5.4-nano. This inflates router prompt tokens.
evidence: window-builder.ts line 107: 'MVP compressed tier: concatenation placeholder'
suggested_action: Wire the nano summarizer call for the compressed tier per plan 02 §5. This is a prompt-length/quality concern rather than a correctness gap.

description: On embedding provider fallback, tool-retriever.ts uses logger.warn instead of emitting the agent_tool_retrieval_fallback_fired_total metric. A TODO comment at line 152 explicitly defers metric emission to plan 07.
evidence: tool-retriever.ts line 152: 'TODO plan 07: replace with metric counter agent_tool_retrieval_fallback_fired_total{cause}'
suggested_action: Wire metric counter once plan 07 metrics infrastructure is in place. Until then, fallback events are only observable via logs.

plan_section: §11 Test Coverage — phase-1/phase-2 fan-out integration tests
description: No integration test exists for the bounded two-phase fan-out (plan 03 §11: 'integration test: 2-phase fan-out with real DB'). The iterative-orchestrator-integration.spec.ts tests the iterative path. No equivalent exists for bounded topology with real DB interactions.
evidence: find agents -name '*.spec.ts' | grep -i 'phase\|bounded\|two-phase\|fan-out' → no results; iterative-orchestrator-integration.spec.ts exists for plan 12 only
suggested_action: Add bounded two-phase fan-out integration test per plan 03 §11 requirements. Must cover: phase-1 parallel fan-out (≤3 sub-agents), partial-answer gate, phase-2 spawning, synthesizer output.

description: pipeline-steps.spec.ts exists and covers the 6-step pipeline. auditEmit never-throw contract is tested. Unit test coverage for the gateway pipeline is present.
evidence: pipeline-steps.spec.ts present alongside pipeline-steps.ts; spec references auditEmit
suggested_action: No action required.

description: router-session-orchestrator.spec.ts exists with integration harness (InMemoryAgentSessionStore). RouterDecisionParser spec also present (router-prompt-builder.spec.ts). Plan 02 test coverage requirements are substantially met.
evidence: spec files found: router-session-orchestrator.spec.ts, router-prompt-builder.spec.ts
suggested_action: No action required.

description: agent_iteration table contains all plan 12 §3 required fields: id, traceId, tenantId, turnId, iterationNumber, subAgentKey, selectionReason, completionScorerResults, isComplete, startedAt, endedAt, usage, taintAtStart. Index on (turnId, iterationNumber) is present.
evidence: agent-iteration.schema.ts: all required fields verified
suggested_action: No action required.
